concepts.title: Spacetime as a Lorentzian manifold
concepts.blurb: Spacetime is a smooth 4-manifold $(M,g)$ with a non-degenerate metric of signature $(-,+,+,+)$. The light cone $g(v,v)=0$ separates timelike, lightlike, and spacelike vectors at each point and pins down the local causal structure: only timelike worldlines can carry massive observers and only causal curves can transmit information.

concepts.title: Einstein field equations
concepts.blurb:
$R_{\mu
u}-	frac12 g_{\mu
u}R+\Lambda g_{\mu
u}=8\pi G\,T_{\mu
u}$ couples the Ricci-trace curvature of spacetime to the stress-energy tensor of matter. Geometry on the left, matter on the right; in vacuum with $\Lambda=0$ the equation reduces to $R_{\mu
u}=0$, a quasi-linear hyperbolic PDE for the metric whose nonlinearity is the source of nearly every interesting GR phenomenon.

concepts.title: Schwarzschild solution and event horizons
concepts.blurb: The unique static spherically-symmetric vacuum solution is $ds^2=-(1-2M/r)\,dt^2+(1-2M/r)^{-1}dr^2+r^2 d\Omega^2$. The coordinate singularity at $r=2M$ is the event horizon — a one-way membrane no causal curve can cross outward — while $r=0$ is a genuine curvature singularity where $R_{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}R^{\mu\nu\rho\sigma}\sim M^2/r^6$.

concepts.title: Kerr metric and rotating black holes
concepts.blurb: A spinning black hole of mass $M$ and angular momentum $J=Ma$ is described by the Kerr metric in Boyer–Lindquist coordinates. Two surfaces appear: the event horizon at $r_+=M+\sqrt{M^2-a^2}$ and an outer stationary-limit surface bounding an ergosphere where no observer can stay at rest — frame-dragging forces co-rotation, enabling the Penrose process for energy extraction.

concepts.title: FLRW cosmology
concepts.blurb: The Friedmann–Lemaître–Robertson–Walker metric $ds^2=-dt^2+a(t)^2[dr^2/(1-kr^2)+r^2 d\Omega^2]$ encodes a homogeneous isotropic universe. Einstein's equations reduce to the Friedmann equations for the scale factor $a(t)$; the redshift $1+z=a(t_0)/a(t_e)$ tracks expansion, and a positive $\Lambda$ drives accelerating expansion as observed for dark energy.

concepts.title: Linearised gravity and gravitational waves
concepts.blurb:
Write $g_{\mu
u}=\eta_{\mu
u}+h_{\mu
u}$ with $|h|\ll1$ on a Minkowski background. In Lorenz gauge the Einstein equations linearise to $\Box  ar h_{\mu
u}=-16\pi G\,T_{\mu
u}$ — a wave equation for two transverse-traceless polarisation modes propagating at $c$. Binary black hole inspirals radiate $\sim 10^{-21}$ strain detected by LIGO via interferometric arms.
